input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on the project name. If your test projects follow a `*.Tests` naming convention:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- properties shared by all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP runner — test projects only -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why `$(MSBuildProjectName)`?** It's available immediately — no dependency on package imports. The often-suggested `$(IsTestProject)` property is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`'s targets, which are imported *after* `Directory.Build.props`, so it isn't reliably available here.

**If your naming isn't consistent**, you can instead define a marker property in each test `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- MyApp.Tests.csproj -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then condition on it in `Directory.Build.targets` (which runs after package props):

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

The `.EndsWith('.Tests')` approach in `Directory.Build.props` is the simplest if your convention holds.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a general MSBuild/dotnet knowledge question — I can answer it directly without needing to explore any codebase.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (or MSTest), or more simply, to condition on the project file name convention (e.g., `*.Tests.csproj`). But the most robust and commonly recommended approach is to check for the `IsTestProject` property, or to use a condition based on whether the project has a test SDK reference.


The cleanest options are conditioning on the project name pattern, using the `IsTestProject` property that `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets automatically, or placing a separate `Directory.Build.props` in a test subfolder. For MTP migration, `IsTestProject` is the most robust choice, though there's a timing consideration with when it gets set.

The issue is that `IsTestProject` gets defined by the test SDK's build props after `Directory.Build.props` evaluates, so it won't be available there. I should use `Directory.Build.targets` instead, which runs after package imports and would have access to `IsTestProject`. Alternatively, I can stick with the naming convention approach in `Directory.Build.props`, or define a custom property in test projects themselves.

The recommended pattern for MTP is conditioning on the project name ending with `.Tests` in `Directory.Build.props`...

Setting MTP properties based on a marker property...
